baseline_source.build: git checkout v0.34.0 && go build -o fak ./cmd/fak && go build -o a2ademo ./cmd/a2ademo
baseline_source.note: Pinned to the v0.34.0 release tag: on 2026-06-26 HEAD was transiently un-buildable (peer commits broke internal/model + internal/loopmgr). v0.34.0 is an ancestor of HEAD and green by construction. Inputs read from the repo tree at run time.

headline.reason: no rule matched; fail-closed default model

systems.pins: the fail-closed default decision (no rule matched -> the safe default model), proving the oracle never fails open
systems.fence: a no-subject request hits the Default by design; supply --aspect/--tool/--manifest for a matched rule (see route-ensemble-refund.json)
systems.compare: re-run and diff JSON (byte-stable) or re-hash against sha256

systems.reproduce: go run ./cmd/fak route --manifest examples/model-routing.example.json --aspect tool_call --tool refund_payment --json
systems.pins: a high-stakes write (refund_payment) routes to a 2-model vote ensemble (guard-a primary + guard-b verifier, reduce=vote)
systems.fence: deterministic policy oracle only; binding members to live model calls is the caller's integration
systems.compare: re-run and diff JSON or re-hash against sha256

systems.pins: on tau2-airline-smoke the vDSO turns 7/12 tool calls into local cache hits -> engine_calls 12->5 (the deterministic ablation delta), bound to workload_hash 9f1701415fb4a360
systems.fence: wall_seconds + p50/p99/mean_ns are environment-measured and VARY; the kernel counters + workload_hash are the stable pin (rung 1 sweeps only the runtime vDSO knob)
systems.compare: re-run and diff the COUNTER fields (engine_calls/vdso_hits/calls/deltas) + workload_hash; ignore the *_ns / wall_seconds latency fields

systems.system: fak vcache prove (M5 planned star-anchor floor)
systems.pins: the deterministic GUARANTEED floor: a star anchor saves 73.39% token-equivalents (21,094.4 of 28,742) across 7 sibling requests
systems.fence: this is the fak-guaranteed planned floor with NO provider; correctness never depends on a provider cache hit
systems.compare: exit 0 = PROVEN; diff saved_pct / saved_token_equiv against baseline

systems.system: fak vcache prove-recall (M4 recall cost gate)
systems.pins: the cost gate REFUSES a single-unit recall-by-rebuild (replay_cost 3000 vs sending cold; break-even = 301 siblings) -> decision cold_prefill
systems.fence: exit 1 is the CORRECT designed outcome (the ~300x loss caught); the gate is OFF by default until the live provider recall loop flips it on
systems.compare: exit 1 = correctly REFUTED; diff replay_cost / break_even_siblings

systems.system: fak vcache prove-telemetry (replay real Codex usage)
systems.pins: replaying the COMMITTED Codex CLI usage (68 events) shows 85.98% realized savings (9,147,340.8 of 10,638,831 token-equiv)
systems.fence: these are OBSERVED numbers relayed from the provider's own cache counters (a realized rebate), not a fak-caused effect; no network (replays a committed file)
systems.compare: exit 0 = PROVEN; diff saved_pct against this baseline for the same input file

systems.system: fak vcache score (2x agent-dev readiness gate)
systems.pins: the composite readiness gate: grade A, score 100, 2x_ready, observed multiplier 7.13x >= 2.00x threshold on the Codex telemetry
systems.fence: the observed multiplier is what that provider delivered on that thread; the planned star-anchor floor (73.4%) is the only fak-guaranteed number
systems.compare: diff grade / score / two_x_better; the planned floor is the stable guaranteed value

systems.pins: the JSON SNAPSHOT SHAPE (schema + keys: process/resource/resource_delta/tasks/concepts) a long-running fak process embeds
systems.fence: resource values (wall/heap/sys/goroutines, *_unix_nano) sample LIVE runtime state and vary every run; pin the shape, not the values
systems.compare: diff the KEY SET / schema (structure), not the resource values

headline.legs: point-to-point ALLOW; capability-floor DENY(TRUST_VIOLATION); pub/sub fan-out; session+window handoff

systems.pins: the deterministic demo trace: an in-kernel mailbox delivers an addressed value gated by the same default-deny floor a tool call rides (a cross-channel private send is DENY TRUST_VIOLATION)
systems.fence: in-process locales + continuation-id handoff are real; durable cross-process delivery is the labeled-STUB next rung
systems.compare: re-run and diff the trace (byte-stable) or re-hash against sha256
